message:
perf(franken): use plain INSERT + UniqueViolation recovery for conversations

The frankensqlite backend was using `INSERT OR IGNORE` for the
`conversations` table to silently skip duplicate
`(source_id, agent_id, external_id)` rows (originally for issue #141).
That worked, but it forced every insert through the OR IGNORE codepath in
fsqlite, which is materially slower than the slim direct-insert lane that
plain `INSERT` now hits after the recent fsqlite-core perf work.

In normal operation virtually every conversation insert is a hit on the
slim path (the row really doesn't exist yet); only the rare concurrent
ingest race collides on the unique provenance index. Paying the OR IGNORE
cost on every insert just to handle the rare collision was backwards.

This commit flips the strategy:

- `franken_insert_conversation` now issues a plain
  `INSERT INTO conversations(...)` without OR IGNORE, so the common miss
  path stays on the direct-insert lane. The function pattern-matches on
  the result:
    * `Ok(_)` → fetch `last_rowid` and return `Ok(Some(id))` as before.
    * `Err(FrankenError::UniqueViolation { .. })` → the concurrent race
      case: log a debug line with the provenance fields and return
      `Ok(None)` so callers can recover the canonical row.
    * Any other error bubbles up unchanged.

- `franken_insert_conversation_or_get_existing` keeps its
  lookup-first-then-insert shape for callers that don't have the
  `PendingConversationKey` already computed, and now delegates the
  after-miss handling to the new helper.

- New `franken_insert_conversation_or_get_existing_after_miss` accepts a
  precomputed `&PendingConversationKey` and skips the redundant
  `franken_find_existing_conversation_by_key` call that the original
  `_or_get_existing` does up front. The two ingest entry points
  (`replay_conversation_pending` and the surrounding append path) already
  compute the conversation key for their own pending-set bookkeeping, so
  routing them through the `_after_miss` helper avoids the duplicate
  lookup entirely. The error message is updated to say "INSERT produced a
  duplicate conflict but existing row was not found" rather than the
  prior OR IGNORE wording.

- The unit test in this file is updated to use the new
  `_after_miss` entry point with a freshly-built
  `conversation_merge_key`, which is the realistic call shape for the
  ingest paths.

Behavior on the wire is unchanged: duplicate provenance still resolves to
the existing canonical row and messages still get merged into it. The only
observable difference is performance — common-case inserts no longer pay
for OR IGNORE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
